name: Marble Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: Vanilla wafer crumbs (1 cup, about 30 wafers)
Cream cheese (3 packages, 8 oz each, softened)
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Chocolate Crumb Crust and set aside.
- Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
- In a medium bowl, stir together crumbs, confectioner's sugar, cocoa, and melted butter. Press mixture onto the bottom and half inch up the sides of a 9-inch springform pan.
- Bake for 8 minutes; cool.
2. Prepare the filling:
- Preheat oven to 450°F (230°C).
- In a large mixing bowl, combine cream cheese, 3/4 cup sugar, sour cream, and 1 teaspoon vanilla; beat on medium speed until smooth.
- Gradually add flour, blending well.
- Add eggs and beat well.
- In a small bowl, combine cocoa and remaining 1/4 cup sugar. Add oil, remaining 1/2 teaspoon vanilla, and half the cream cheese mixture; blend well.
- Pour plain and chocolate mixtures alternately into the cooled crust, ending with dollops of chocolate on top. Swirl gently with a metal spatula or knife to create a marble effect.
3. Bake for 10 minutes at 450°F (230°C).
4. Without opening the oven door, decrease temperature to 250°F (130°C) and continue baking for an additional 30 minutes.
5. Turn off the oven; leave the cheesecake in the oven for another 30 minutes without opening the door.
6. Remove from the oven; loosen the cake from the side of the pan. Cool completely before serving.
